extra.reasoning: The property requires that flipping confusion from false to true (all other inputs unchanged) never lowers acuity. Consider the baseline where every input is true EXCEPT confusion=false. In that baseline, r6 (priority 100) does not fire because it requires confusion=true; instead the priority-3 emergent rules fire (r1 pallor, r5 dyspnea, r9 vision_loss, r11 chest_pain, r13 syncope), so acuity = emergent (the highest level). Now set confusion=true with everything else unchanged (the witness_assignment: all 13 inputs true). Now r6's full conjunction is satisfied, and with priority 100 it dominates every priority-3 rule, setting acuity := self_care (the lowest level in the order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent]). Thus flipping confusion true drops acuity from emergent to self_care — a strict decrease. confusion appears in no other rule and only ever appears in r6, which outputs the lowest acuity at the highest priority, so it can only lower (never raise) acuity. The monotonicity property is violated. The witness_assignment is the post-flip input (confusion=true, all others true); the comparison baseline is the identical input with confusion=false, which yields emergent.
